author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! ebe tora e kemana rīta
je karme āili hethā, se⋅saba rahila kothā,
ebe je dekhiye viparīta

kṛṣṇa⋅karma lāgi’ kara, tāhe kena barbvara,
se⋅kare parera vitta hara
se avaśa nahe kene, ki susāra bahu dāne,
tāhe āra kara vā nā kara

mukhe ka’be ‘hṛṣīkeśa’, tāhe jadi sādhu⋅dveṣa,
tabe bakramukha kene noo
agni diyā hena mukha, poḍā’le nā ghuce dukha,
tāhe ‘kṛṣṇa’ koo vā nā koo

bhramite kṛṣṇera tīrtha, padera nā ehi kṛtya,
tāhe jadi para⋅dāre cala
ki kāja padera ei, paṅgu kene nahe sei,
tabe tīrthe gela vā nā gela

kṛṣṇa⋅līlā⋅guṇa⋅kathā, karṇete śunibe jathā,
tāhe jadi ku⋅kathāya bhora
jadi āra sādhu⋅nindā, śuniyā bāḍaye śraddhā,
se kāṇa badhira hou tora

guru⋅kṛṣṇa⋅vaiṣṇava⋅mūrti, dekhibe kariyā ārti,
se jadi dekhaye para⋅dāre
asantoṣa sādhu dekhi’, kena vidhi hena ā̐khi,
āśu andha nā kare tāhāre

tumi kṛṣṇa⋅smṛti⋅kāje, janmilā saṁsāra⋅mājhe,
tāhā chāḍi’ dhane⋅jane āśa
tabe jīye kibā kāja, paḍuka tora muṇḍe bāja,
kene āra nahe sarvanāśa

premānanda kahe mana, ‘kṛṣṇa’ kaha anukṣaṇa,
kene bhula āpanāra prabhu
mukhe ‘hari hari’ bala, sadāi ānande dola,
tina⋅loke duḥkha nahe kabhu
en_translation:
O my mind! What kind of conduct is this of yours now? The spiritual deeds for which you came here—where have they all gone? Now you show only the opposite.

You are meant to engage in Lord Kṛṣṇa's service—why then are you so barbaric? You spend your time stealing the wealth of others. Why are you not subdued? What good is giving much in charity if your heart is not right?

With your mouth you say "Hṛṣīkeśa," but if there is hatred for the saintly in your heart, then why is your mouth not crooked? Even if such a mouth is burned with fire, the sorrow does not end, whether you say "Kṛṣṇa" or not.

To wander to the holy places of Lord Kṛṣṇa is the duty of your feet—but if with them you go to the wife of another, what is the use of those feet? Why are they not lame? Whether they went to holy places or not would then make no difference.

The pastimes and qualities of Lord Kṛṣṇa should be heard with the ears—but if instead you are engrossed in evil talk, or if by hearing blasphemy of the saintly your faith increases, then may those ears of yours be deaf.

You should look upon the forms of the Guru, Kṛṣṇa, and the devotees with earnest longing—but if instead your eyes look with lust at the wife of another, or if you look upon a saintly person with dissatisfaction, why does destiny not quickly make you blind?

You were born into this material world for the purpose of remembering Lord Kṛṣṇa, but leaving that aside, you only desire wealth and people. Then what is the use of living? May thunder strike your head; why is there not total destruction?

Premānanda says: "O my mind! Chant 'Kṛṣṇa!' always. Why do you forget your own Lord? Say 'Hari! Hari!' with your mouth and always sway in bliss—then there will never be any sorrow in the three worlds."
